title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a BST
problem_statement: Find the lowest common ancestor of two nodes in a binary search tree.

examples.explanation: 6 is the lowest node that has both 2 and 8 as descendants.

constraints: All node values are unique and p, q exist in the tree.
intuition: Use BST ordering: if both nodes are less than root, go left; if both are greater, go right.

approaches.name: BST property traversal
approaches.description: Walk down the tree until the split point or root equals one of the nodes.
approaches.why_not: General binary tree LCA methods work but are more expensive than using BST properties.

interview_qa.a: When one node is on the left and the other is on the right, or one equals the current root.
It lets you choose the correct subtree without exploring both sides.
